data.title: KingClean Electric to Dispose of Controlling Stake in Agricultural Machinery-Making Subsidiary
EQT to Buy Majority Stake in Filtration Services Provider Desotec from Blackstone
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping


STOCKHOLM--EQT agreed to buy a majority stake in filtration services provider Desotec from Blackstone, it said Friday.

The Swedish buyout group said it is partnering with two German family offices, Athos and Merckle, who will co-invest alongside EQT, while Blackstone will remain a minority investor.

Belgium-based Desotec provides services that remove pollutants from air, water and soil.

EQT and Blackstone plan to support Desotec's continued growth across Europe and to accelerate its expansion in North America, where the market for mobile filtration is still in its early stages.

The transaction is subject to customary conditions and approvals and is expected to close during the first half of 2026.

Financial terms weren't disclosed.


Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-21-25 0239ET

Italy closes Google probe over unfair use of personal data after remedies adopted
data.description:
MILAN (Reuters) -Italy's competition authority said on Friday it had closed a probe it had launched last year over Google's alleged unfair commercial practices in the use of personal data after the online search giant adopted remedies.

In July 2024 the authority opened an investigation as the request Google sent its users for consent to connect to its services "could constitute a misleading and aggressive commercial practice", and that the information it provided was "incomplete and misleading".

After the regulator's probe, Google will change its consent request "by providing clearer and more accurate information on the implications of consent for the use of personal data", as well as on the range and volume of its services where consent may involve the "combination" and "cross-use" of personal data, the Antitrust said in statement.

(Reporting by Gianluca Semeraro, editing by Giulia Segreti)

Air cargo services between India, Afghanistan to be launched soon
data.description:
NEW DELHI (Reuters) -Air cargo services between India and Afghanistan will be launched soon, an Indian foreign ministry official said on Friday, during a visit by Afghanistan's Taliban Trade Minister Al-Haj Nooruddin Azizi to New Delhi.

"I am pleased to announce that the air freight corridor on the Kabul-Delhi sector and Kabul-Amristar routes have been activated and cargo flights on these sectors will come very soon," the official, Anand Prakash, said.

(Reporting by Nigam Prusty; Writing by Sakshi Dayal; Editing by YP Rajesh)

data.description:
OSLO (Reuters) -Swedish fashion retailer H&M said on Friday it has decided to buy back its own shares on the stock market for 1 billion Swedish crowns ($104.83 million).

($1 = 9.5396 Swedish crowns)

H&M to Repurchase Own Shares for Up to 1 Billion SEK
data.description:
Fashion retailer H&M's board has decided to utilize the authorization granted by the annual general meeting in May to repurchase its own shares, according to a press release.

The company will buy back shares for a maximum of 1 billion Swedish kronor. The program begins today, November 21, and will run until no later than January 28 next year.

The purpose of the buyback is to distribute surplus liquidity and thereby adjust H&M's capital structure by reducing capital.

Accordingly, the board intends to propose at the 2026 annual general meeting that the repurchased shares be canceled. The reduction in share capital is expected to be offset by a corresponding bonus issue, so that the size of the share capital is restored.

data.title: Oracle Corporation Names Stephen Rusckowski to the Board of Directors, Effective on November 18, 2025
data.description:
Oracle Corporation announced that it unanimously elected Stephen Rusckowski to Oracle's Board of Directors and increased the size of the Board to 14. The election is effective as of November 18, 2025. Mr. Rusckowski is the former Chief Executive Officer and President of Quest Diagnostics Inc. (Quest), a leading provider of diagnostic information, a position he held from 2012 until 2022.

He also served as Chair of the Board of Quest from January 2017 through March 2023. Prior to joining Quest, he served as the Chief Executive Officer of Philips Healthcare, and a member of the Board of Management of Royal Philips Electronics. Mr. Rusckowski currently serves on the supervisory board of Qiagen N.V. and as a director of Baxter International Inc. Mr. Rusckowski earned a Bachelor of Science degree in Mechanical Engineering from Worcester Polytechnic Institute and a Master of Science degree in Management from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology'sSloan School of Management.

Members of Oracle's Board of Directors serve one-year terms and will next stand for election at the company's annual meeting of stockholders in November 2026.

Three killed after 5.7 magnitude quake in Bangladesh, police says
data.description:
DHAKA (Reuters) -At least three people were killed and several injured after a magnitude 5.7 earthquake struck Bangladesh on Friday, police said.

(Reporting by Ruma Paul; Editing by YP Rajesh)
